text.content:
GM Utility Macros

Two macros ship in the Blooming Rot 2 — Utility Macros compendium. Both are GM-only and persist state in world settings, so data survives between sessions.

BR2 — Phase Clock Tracker

Tracks the two campaign-level clocks that run continuously across all five surface phases:

  • Haskur Trail Clock (0–6) — how close the trail to Haskur Vandrell is to going cold. Advances when the party loses a lead, a witness is silenced before being reached, or the party spends a full phase without forward motion toward the primary target.
  • Greyhawk Heat Clock (0–6) — how much attention the Aerdy commercial-court network is paying to the party’s movements. Advances when the party acts publicly in ways the network can observe.

Open the macro, adjust the numbers, click Save. A whispered chat summary appears with current values. Use Show only to report without changing anything.

These clocks are separate from the Phase 2 pressure clock (the six-segment Loftwick investigation clock), which is tracked manually. See Phase 2 — Quick Reference (GM) for the full pressure clock table and advancement triggers.

BR2 — Campaign State Snapshot

Tracks the carry-forward state of four major threads and five active lies. These variables are read by Phase 6 to determine which ending configuration the party reaches.

Thread fields

FieldWhat it tracksDefault
SerethThe fate of Sereth Vaun and the civil-court case. Path A = publicly indicted via the Audit Commission; Path B = quietly removed before trial; Path C = kinetically removed (killed or disappeared) by party action or conspiracy response.Unresolved
Closure motionThe Council of Barons motion to close the Audit Hall’s Phase 2 inquiry. Passed = shut down; Held over = Caelith bought time; Defeated = motion failed outright.Unresolved
PellThe fate of the conspiracy’s inside contact in the Audit Hall. Exposed = unmasked publicly; Turned = flipped as an asset; Fed false information = actively disinformed; Shadowed = party watching without tipping hand; Removed = extracted from position; Killed.Unresolved
Vesten QuillThe outcome of the false-manuscript thread into the Aerdy network. Captured = party has Quill in hand; Escaped = Quill got away; Never reached = party didn’t pursue this thread.Unresolved

Active lies

Check a lie when the party has deliberately and successfully planted it with the conspiracy’s intelligence layer — not merely said it aloud, but ensured it reached the network. Each active lie shifts the conspiracy’s response in Phase 5 and 6.

#LieEffect if active in Phase 6
1Haskur in Rel AstraNetwork pulls Greyhawk assets east; party has an easier approach window.
2Party pursuing wood correspondentNetwork watches lumber-trade routes, not the commercial-court filing route.
3Party has no usable Sereth evidenceNetwork does not activate the document-destruction protocol in Phase 6.
4Party intends to expose Galenix NaelaxNetwork moves to protect Naelax, pulling coverage away from Haskur’s operation.
5Party returning to HardbyGreyhawk tail is stood down; party enters the city unwatched.

When to update

Update at the end of any session where a thread resolves or materially changes. Run Save + Report to save and whisper a summary to GM chat.

Welcome to Blooming Rot, Part 2

A six-phase Greyhawk adventure for D&D 2024, set in the pre-Wars Yeomanry, Hardby, Rel Astra, and Greyhawk City. For 4–6 PCs, levels 7–11. Approximately 18–25 sessions if every sandbox branch is engaged, or 12–15 sessions on the main thread.

The investigation in one paragraph

Caelith Dunivar, the Yeomanry's quiet spymaster, has buried a body in his own city's strong-room: forty pages of foreign correspondence, a dead man's ledger, a sealed packet that smells of an ink no Yeomanry house uses. He summons the party, hands them the case, and asks them to follow it. Across five surface phases they trace a paper trail through Loftwick, Hardby, Rel Astra, and Greyhawk City. By Phase 6, they have the man at the end of the trail in their hands — only to learn the man is a door, the door is rented, and the path beyond it descends.

How this module is organised

Compendium packContents
Phase 1 — 5 JournalsThe narrative content of the surface investigation. Read aloud blocks, GM notes, scene-by-scene structure.
Phase 1 — 5 ActorsNPC stat blocks for every named character. Most use MM 2024 archetypes; the major figures (Caelith, Trina, Sereth, etc.) have bespoke stat blocks.
Phase 1 — ScenesPre-built Foundry Scenes for Phase 1 with battlemaps loaded. (Phases 2–6 will follow.)
Phase 6 — Journals + ActorsThe Greyhawk City phase. New as of v0.7.
Sandbox — Journals + ActorsPer-PC anchor NPCs and downtime references for Loftwick, Hardby, Rel Astra, and Greyhawk City. Use between phases or whenever the party has a free day.

How to read the GM-facing materials

  • Bordered cream-tinted blockquotes are read-aloud / read-to-the-table text. You can read them verbatim or paraphrase.
  • Prose paragraphs are GM-facing explanation, mechanics, and judgment calls.
  • Tables are reference data — carry-forward variables, NPC quick reads, clock advancement triggers.
  • The phase chapters in docs/blooming-rot-2-gm-prep.pdf reproduce all of this in a single ~360-page printable document, with flow diagrams, NPC profile cards, and inline illustrations — designed for session-by-session prep ahead of game day.

Foundry-specific tips

  • Compendium imports: drag any actor or journal into the world to use it. NPCs come with full stat blocks, items linked to PHB/DMG/MM 2024, and round metal-ringed tokens already wired.
  • Items are pre-linked to the official D&D 2024 modules where the party will recognise them. If you have Player's Handbook, Dungeon Master's Guide, and Monster Manual installed, weapons render with proper masteries (Vex, Sap, Nick, etc.) automatically.
  • Maps: keyed location maps for every important interior live in the module's assets/maps/ folder. Drop them into a Scene at 5 ft per square.
  • Handouts: Phase 6's Ashen Itinerary ships in three fidelity states (intact, partially burned, scorched fragments) so the GM can match the climax outcome. Other handouts (registry pages, ledger excerpts, road notes, manuscript analyses) are in the Phase 6 — Player Handouts journal.

Reading order for the GM

  1. Read this Welcome.
  2. Read Phase 1 — Return to Loftwick in full. The first three sessions are tightly scripted; everything afterward grows from how the players handle them.
  3. Read Phase 2 — Departure and the Sandbox Reference for the city the party is in.
  4. Before each session, re-read the phase chapter for that session's expected scene. Each phase chapter has a flow diagram showing where decision points branch.
  5. Trust the carry-forward tables. Phase 6 specifically reads what the party did in Phase 5 (Sereth's path, Closure motion, Pell's fate, Vesten Quill's outcome, Lies 1–5).

If something breaks

If the party misses a clue, ignore a node, or fail-forward the climax, the campaign continues. Every phase has at least one fallback path; even total tactical failure in the Stonecistern leaves the route mark on the wall.

If a party member captures, kills, or fails to catch Haskur Vandrell, the campaign continues. He is not authorially protected.

If a Foundry import looks broken, reach out: github.com/morrigangirl/blooming-rot-2/issues.

What's coming

  • Phase 7 — The Seventh Question (working title): translate the Phase 6 evidence and locate the surface approach to the deep route.
  • Phase 8 — The Threshold: the surface threshold and upper descent.
  • Phase 9: the first true below-ground phase.

The campaign should not name the Underdark, Eclavdra, House Eilserv, the Vault, the Demonweb, or Lolth in player-facing text until Phase 8 at the earliest. Restraint at the table makes the threshold land.

Credits

Adventure design and writing: Aoibh Wood. Mechanical assist (D&D 2024 PHB/DMG/MM linkage, NPC archetypes): the official 2024 books, with thanks to Wizards of the Coast.

Begin in Phase 1. Caelith is waiting.
